questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司運行高度可用的 SFTP 服務。 SFTP 服務使用兩個使用彈性 IP 位址運作的 Amazon EC2 Linux 執行個體來接受來自網際網路上受信任 IP 來源的流量。 SFTP 服務由附加到執行個體的共用儲存提供支援。使用者帳戶在 SFTP 伺服器中以 Linux 使用者身分建立和管理。該公司想要一種能夠提供高 IOPS 效能和高度可設定安全性的無伺服器選項。該公司還希望保持對用戶權限的控制。哪種解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company runs a highly available SFTP service. The SFTP service uses two Amazon EC2 Linux instances that run with elastic IP addresses to accept traffic from trusted IP sources on the internet. The SFTP service is backed by shared storage that is attached to the instances. User accounts are created and managed as Linux users in the SFTP servers. The company wants a serverless option that provides high IOPS performance and highly configurable security. The company also wants to maintain control over user permissions. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 建立加密的 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 磁碟區。使用僅允許受信任 IP 位址的公共終端節點建立 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服務。將 EBS 磁碟區附加到 SFTP 服務端點。授予使用者存取 SFTP 服務的權限。
A.en: Create an encrypted Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume. Create an AWS Transfer Family SFTP service with a public endpoint that allows only trusted IP addresses. Attach the EBS volume to the SFTP service endpoint. Grant users access to the SFTP service.

B.zh: 建立加密的 Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) 磁碟區。使用彈性 IP 位址和具有面向 Internet 存取權限的 VPC 終端節點建立 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服務。將安全群組附加到僅允許受信任 IP 位址的端點。將 EFS 磁碟區附加到 SFTP 服務端點。授予使用者存取 SFTP 服務的權限。
B.en: Create an encrypted Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) volume. Create an AWS Transfer Family SFTP service with elastic IP addresses and a VPC endpoint that has internet-facing access. Attach a security group to the endpoint that allows only trusted IP addresses. Attach the EFS volume to the SFTP service endpoint. Grant users access to the SFTP service.

C.zh: 建立啟用預設加密的 Amazon S3 儲存桶。使用僅允許受信任 IP 位址的公共終端節點建立 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服務。將 S3 儲存桶附加到 SFTP 服務端點。授予使用者存取 SFTP 服務的權限。
C.en: Create an Amazon S3 bucket with default encryption enabled. Create an AWS Transfer Family SFTP service with a public endpoint that allows only trusted IP addresses. Attach the S3 bucket to the SFTP service endpoint. Grant users access to the SFTP service.

D.zh: 建立啟用預設加密的 Amazon S3 儲存桶。使用在私有子網路中具有內部存取權限的 VPC 終端節點建立 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服務。附加僅允許受信任 IP 位址的安全群組。將 S3 儲存桶附加到 SFTP 服務端點。授予使用者存取 SFTP 服務的權限。
D.en: Create an Amazon S3 bucket with default encryption enabled. Create an AWS Transfer Family SFTP service with a VPC endpoint that has internal access in a private subnet. Attach a security group that allows only trusted IP addresses. Attach the S3 bucket to the SFTP service endpoint. Grant users access to the SFTP service.

A.zh: 錯誤。建立加密的 Amazon EBS 磁碟區並使用 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服務：Amazon EBS 是區塊存儲，通常用於單一 EC2 實例，而不是共用儲存。 AWS Transfer Family 無法直接附加 EBS 磁碟區。此選項不滿足高效能共享儲存的要求，因此是一個。
A.en: Incorrect. Create an encrypted Amazon EBS volume and use the AWS Transfer Family SFTP service: Amazon EBS is block storage and is typically used for individual EC2 instances, not shared storage. AWS Transfer Family cannot attach EBS volumes directly. This option does not meet the requirements for High Performance Shared Storage and is therefore an.

B.zh: 正確。建立加密的 Amazon EFS 磁碟區並使用 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服務：Amazon EFS 是一種共用檔案存儲，支援高 IOPS 效能，適合多執行個體存取。 AWS Transfer Family可以透過VPC端點與EFS集成，以滿足「高效能」和「安全」的要求。
B.en: Correct. Create an encrypted Amazon EFS volume and use the AWS Transfer Family SFTP service: Amazon EFS is a shared file storage that supports high IOPS performance and is suitable for multi-instance access. AWS Transfer Family can be integrated with EFS through VPC endpoints to meet the requirements of "high performance" and "security".

C.zh: 錯誤。建立 Amazon S3 儲存桶並使用 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服務：Amazon S3 是虛擬儲存。雖然支援AWS Transfer Family，但不適合高IOPS場景。該選項不滿足「高能量儲存」的要求，因此是一個。
C.en: Incorrect. Create an Amazon S3 bucket and use the AWS Transfer Family SFTP service: Amazon S3 is a virtual storage. Although it supports AWS Transfer Family, it is not suitable for high IOPS scenarios. This option does not meet the requirements for "high energy storage" and is therefore an.

D.zh: 錯誤。建立 Amazon S3 儲存桶並使用 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服務（私有子網路存取）：與選項 C 類似，Amazon S3 不適合高 IOPS 場景。雖然安全資料庫配置正確，但儲存類型不符合要求，因此。
D.en: Incorrect. Create an Amazon S3 bucket and use the AWS Transfer Family SFTP service (private subnet access): Similar to option C, Amazon S3 is not suitable for high IOPS scenarios. Although the security database is configured correctly, the storage type does not meet the requirements, hence the.

answer_text.zh: B. 建立加密的 Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) 磁碟區。使用彈性 IP 位址和具有面向 Internet 存取權限的 VPC 終端節點建立 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服務。將安全群組附加到僅允許受信任 IP 位址的端點。將 EFS 磁碟區附加到 SFTP 服務端點。授予使用者存取 SFTP 服務的權限。
answer_text.en: B. Create an encrypted Amazon Elastic File System (Amazon EFS) volume. Create an AWS Transfer Family SFTP service with elastic IP addresses and a VPC endpoint that has internet-facing access. Attach a security group to the endpoint that allows only trusted IP addresses. Attach the EFS volume to the SFTP service endpoint. Grant users access to the SFTP service.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (85%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：建立加密的 Amazon EFS 磁碟區並使用 AWS Transfer Family SFTP 服務：Amazon EFS 是一種共用檔案存儲，支援高 IOPS 效能，適合多執行個體存取。 AWS Transfer Family可以透過VPC端點與EFS集成，以滿足「高效能」和「安全」的要求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (85%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Create an encrypted Amazon EFS volume and use the AWS Transfer Family SFTP service: Amazon EFS is a shared file storage that supports high IOPS performance and is suitable for multi-instance access. AWS Transfer Family can be integrated with EFS through VPC endpoints to meet the requirements of "high performance" and "security".

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在 AWS 上開發新的機器學習 (ML) 模型解決方案。這些模型被開發為獨立的微服務，在啟動時從 Amazon S3 獲取大約 1 GB 的模型資料並將資料載入記憶體。使用者透過非同步 API 存取模型。用戶可以發送一個請求或一批請求，並指定結果應發送到何處。該公司為數百名用戶提供模型。模型的使用模式是不規則的。某些型號可能會閒置數天或數週。其他模型可以一次接收數千個批次的請求。解決方案架構師應該推薦哪一種設計來滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company is developing a new machine learning (ML) model solution on AWS. The models are developed as independent microservices that fetch approximately 1 GB of model data from Amazon S3 at startup and load the data into memory. Users access the models through an asynchronous API. Users can send a request or a batch of requests and specify where the results should be sent. The company provides models to hundreds of users. The usage patterns for the models are irregular. Some models could be unused for days or weeks. Other models could receive batches of thousands of requests at a time. Which design should a solutions architect recommend to meet these requirements?

A.zh: 將來自 API 的請求導向至網路負載平衡器 (NLB)。將模型部署為 NLB 呼叫的 AWS Lambda 函數。
A.en: Direct the requests from the API to a Network Load Balancer (NLB). Deploy the models as AWS Lambda functions that are invoked by the NLB.

B.zh: 將請求從 API 導向至應用程式負載平衡器 (ALB)。將模型部署為從 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 佇列讀取的 Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 服務。使用 AWS App Mesh 根據 SQS 佇列大小擴充 ECS 叢集的執行個體。
B.en: Direct the requests from the API to an Application Load Balancer (ALB). Deploy the models as Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) services that read from an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Use AWS App Mesh to scale the instances of the ECS cluster based on the SQS queue size.

C.zh: 將來自 API 的請求導向至 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 佇列。將模型部署為由 SQS 事件呼叫的 AWS Lambda 函數。使用 AWS Auto Scaling 根據 SQS 佇列大小增加 Lambda 函數的 vCPU 數量。
C.en: Direct the requests from the API into an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Deploy the models as AWS Lambda functions that are invoked by SQS events. Use AWS Auto Scaling to increase the number of vCPUs for the Lambda functions based on the SQS queue size.

D.zh: 將來自 API 的請求導向至 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 佇列。將模型部署為從佇列中讀取的 Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 服務。根據佇列大小為叢集和服務副本在 Amazon ECS 上啟用 AWS Auto Scaling。
D.en: Direct the requests from the API into an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Deploy the models as Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) services that read from the queue. Enable AWS Auto Scaling on Amazon ECS for both the cluster and copies of the service based on the queue size.

A.zh: 錯誤。Lambda函數不適合處理1GB模型資料載入到記憶體的場景，因為Lambda的記憶體和執行時間有限（最多15分鐘）。此外，NLB 通常用於處理 TCP/UDP 流量，而不是非同步 API 請求。
A.en: Incorrect. The Lambda function is not suitable for processing scenarios where 1GB of model data is loaded into memory, because Lambda has limited memory and execution time (maximum 15 minutes). Additionally, NLB is typically used to handle TCP/UDP traffic, not asynchronous API requests.

B.zh: 錯誤。雖然 ALB 和 ECS 的組合是有效的，但使用 AWS App Mesh 來擴充 ECS 執行個體並不是最佳選擇。 AWS App Mesh主要用於服務間通訊而非直接擴充ECS實例。但是，在此場景中不需要使用 ALB，因為請求已透過 SQS 佇列非同步處理。
B.en: Incorrect. Although the combination of ALB and ECS is effective, using AWS App Mesh to scale ECS instances is not the best choice. AWS App Mesh is mainly used for inter-service communication rather than directly extending ECS ​​instances. However, the use of ALB is not necessary in this scenario because the requests are already processed asynchronously through the SQS queue.

C.zh: 錯誤。Lambda函數仍然不適合處理1GB模型資料載入到記憶體的場景。 Lambda 的記憶體和執行時間限制使其不切實際。此外，Lambda透過Auto Scaling直接增加vCPU數量。
C.en: Incorrect. The Lambda function is still not suitable for handling the scenario where 1GB of model data is loaded into memory. Lambda's memory and execution time constraints make it impractical. In addition, Lambda directly increases the number of vCPUs through Auto Scaling.

D.zh: 正確。此選項使用 SQS 佇列處理非同步 API 請求並透過 ECS 服務載入模型資料。 ECS支援大規模資料載入到記憶體的場景，並且可以透過AWS Auto Scaling根據佇列大小動態擴展服務執行個體和叢集容量。這種設計滿足了不規則的使用模式和高並發請求的需求。
D.en: Correct. this option uses an SQS queue to process asynchronous API requests and loads model data through the ECS service. ECS supports scenarios where large-scale data is loaded into memory, and service instances and cluster capacity can be dynamically expanded based on queue size through AWS Auto Scaling. This design meets the needs of irregular usage patterns and high concurrency requests.

answer_text.zh: D. 將來自 API 的請求導向至 Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) 佇列。將模型部署為從佇列中讀取的 Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 服務。根據佇列大小為叢集和服務副本在 Amazon ECS 上啟用 AWS Auto Scaling。
answer_text.en: D. Direct the requests from the API into an Amazon Simple Queue Service (Amazon SQS) queue. Deploy the models as Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) services that read from the queue. Enable AWS Auto Scaling on Amazon ECS for both the cluster and copies of the service based on the queue size.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：D (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：此選項使用 SQS 佇列處理非同步 API 請求並透過 ECS 服務載入模型資料。 ECS支援大規模資料載入到記憶體的場景，並且可以透過AWS Auto Scaling根據佇列大小動態擴展服務執行個體和叢集容量。這種設計滿足了不規則的使用模式和高並發請求的需求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: this option uses an SQS queue to process asynchronous API requests and loads model data through the ECS service. ECS supports scenarios where large-scale data is loaded into memory, and service instances and cluster capacity can be dynamically expanded based on queue size through AWS Auto Scaling. This design meets the needs of irregular usage patterns and high concurrency requests.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 解決方案架構師想要使用下列 JSON 文字作為基於身分的策略來授予特定權限：解決方案架構師可以將此策略附加到哪些 IAM 主體？ （選兩個。）
question_text.en: A solutions architect wants to use the following JSON text as an identity-based policy to grant specific permissions: Which IAM principals can the solutions architect attach this policy to? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 角色
A.en: Role

B.zh: 組
B.en: Group

C.zh: 組織
C.en: Organization

D.zh: Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) 資源
D.en: Amazon Elastic Container Service (Amazon ECS) resource

A.zh: 正確。。 IAM 角色是可以附加身分策略的主體。身份策略可以直接為角色授予權限。
A.en: Correct. . An IAM role is a principal to which identity policies can be attached. Identity policies can directly grant permissions to roles.

B.zh: 正確。。 IAM 使用者群組是可以附加身分原則的委託人。使用者群組中的所有使用者都繼承該群組附加的權限。
B.en: Correct. . An IAM user group is a principal to which identity policies can be attached. All users in a user group inherit the permissions attached to the group.

C.zh: 錯誤。。 AWS Organizations 是一項管理多個 AWS 帳戶的服務，無法直接附加身分識別原則。策略需要透過服務控制策略 (SCP) 進行管理，而不是身分識別策略。
C.en: Incorrect. . AWS Organizations is a service that manages multiple AWS accounts and cannot directly attach identity policies. Policies need to be managed through Service Control Policies (SCPs), not identity policies.

D.zh: 錯誤。。 Amazon ECS 資源屬於服務資源，不能直接附加身分策略。資源權限通常透過資源策略或角色進行管理。
D.en: Incorrect. . Amazon ECS resources are service resources and cannot be directly attached with identity policies. Resource permissions are typically managed through resource policies or roles.

E.zh: 錯誤。。 Amazon EC2 資源屬於服務資源，無法直接附加身分策略。資源權限通常透過資源策略或角色進行管理。
E.en: Incorrect. . Amazon EC2 resources are service resources and cannot be directly attached with identity policies. Resource permissions are typically managed through resource policies or roles.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：AB (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：。 IAM 角色是可以附加身分策略的主體。身份策略可以直接為角色授予權限。；。 IAM 使用者群組是可以附加身分原則的委託人。使用者群組中的所有使用者都繼承該群組附加的權限。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: AB (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: . An IAM role is a principal to which identity policies can be attached. Identity policies can directly grant permissions to roles.; . An IAM user group is a principal to which identity policies can be attached. All users in a user group inherit the permissions attached to the group.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在 Amazon EC2 隨選執行個體上執行自訂應用程式。應用程式的前端節點需要每週 7 天、每天 24 小時運行，而後端節點則根據工作負載僅需要運行很短的時間。後端節點的數量在一天中會有所不同。公司需要根據工作負載橫向擴展和擴展更多實例。哪一種解決方案能夠最具成本效益地滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company is running a custom application on Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances. The application has frontend nodes that need to run 24 hours a day, 7 days a week and backend nodes that need to run only for a short time based on workload. The number of backend nodes varies during the day. The company needs to scale out and scale in more instances based on workload. Which solution will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 前端節點使用預留實例。使用 AWS Fargate 作為後端節點。
A.en: Use Reserved Instances for the frontend nodes. Use AWS Fargate for the backend nodes.

B.zh: 前端節點使用預留實例。使用 Spot 實例作為後端節點。
B.en: Use Reserved Instances for the frontend nodes. Use Spot Instances for the backend nodes.

C.zh: 對前端節點使用 Spot 實例。對後端節點使用預留實例。
C.en: Use Spot Instances for the frontend nodes. Use Reserved Instances for the backend nodes.

D.zh: 前端節點使用 Spot 實例。使用 AWS Fargate 作為後端節點。
D.en: Use Spot Instances for the frontend nodes. Use AWS Fargate for the backend nodes.

A.zh: 錯誤。預留執行個體適合連續運作的前端，但 Fargate 並不是問題中所述的可變可中斷 EC2 後端的最低成本選擇。
A.en: Incorrect. Reserved Instances fit the continuously running frontend, but Fargate is not the lowest-cost choice for the variable interruptible EC2 backend described in the question.

B.zh: 正確。預留執行個體可降低穩定前端工作負載的成本，而 Spot 執行個體可為隨需求擴展的短期後端容量提供最低的 EC2 成本。
B.en: Correct. Reserved Instances reduce the cost of the steady frontend workload, while Spot Instances provide the lowest EC2 cost for short-lived backend capacity that scales with demand.

C.zh: 錯誤。Spot 執行個體可能會中斷連續需要的前端，而預留執行個體與僅間歇運作的後端容量相符不佳。
C.en: Incorrect. Spot Instances can interrupt the continuously required frontend, while Reserved Instances are poorly matched to backend capacity that runs only intermittently.

D.zh: 錯誤。Spot 執行個體無法為始終在線的前端提供適當的連續性，且 Fargate 並不是此 EC2 工作負載最具成本效益的後端選擇。
D.en: Incorrect. Spot Instances do not provide appropriate continuity for the always-on frontend, and Fargate is not the most cost-effective backend choice for this EC2 workload.

answer_text.zh: B. 前端節點使用預留實例。使用 Spot 實例作為後端節點。
answer_text.en: B. Use Reserved Instances for the frontend nodes. Use Spot Instances for the backend nodes.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (62%)； A (38%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：預留執行個體可降低穩定前端工作負載的成本，而 Spot 執行個體可為隨需求擴展的短期後端容量提供最低的 EC2 成本。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (62%); A (38%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Reserved Instances reduce the cost of the steady frontend workload, while Spot Instances provide the lowest EC2 cost for short-lived backend capacity that scales with demand.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司使用高區塊儲存容量在本地運行其工作負載。公司每天每秒的峰值輸入輸出交易不超過15,000 IOPS。該公司希望將工作負載遷移到 Amazon EC2，並獨立於儲存容量配置磁碟效能。哪種 Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) 磁碟區類型能夠最經濟高效地滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company uses high block storage capacity to runs its workloads on premises. The company's daily peak input and output transactions per second are not more than 15,000 IOPS. The company wants to migrate the workloads to Amazon EC2 and to provision disk performance independent of storage capacity. Which Amazon Elastic Block Store (Amazon EBS) volume type will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: GP2 卷類型
A.en: GP2 volume type

B.zh: io2 卷類型
B.en: io2 volume type

C.zh: GP3 卷類型
C.en: GP3 volume type

D.zh: io1 卷類型
D.en: io1 volume type

A.zh: 錯誤。GP2磁碟區類型的效能與儲存容量有關，IOPS隨著儲存容量的增加而增加。雖然GP2可以支援100 IOPS，但其效能無法獨立於儲存容量進行配置，因此無法滿足問題要求。
A.en: Incorrect. The performance of the GP2 volume type is related to the storage capacity, and IOPS increases as the storage capacity increases. Although GP2 can support 100 IOPS, its performance cannot be configured independently of storage capacity, so it does not meet the question requirements.

B.zh: 錯誤。io2卷類型提供高性能和高耐用性，支援獨立配置IOPS，但成本較高。問題要求「最具成本效益」的解決方案，因此io2不是最佳選擇。
B.en: Incorrect. The io2 volume type provides high performance and high durability, and supports independent configuration of IOPS, but the cost is high. The question asks for the "most cost-effective" solution, so io2 is not the best choice.

C.zh: 正確。GP3 磁碟區類型支援獨立配置 IOPS 和吞吐量，無論儲存容量為何。 GP3 的成本低於 io1 和 io2，並且可以處理 15,000 IOPS，使其成為最具成本效益的選擇。
C.en: Correct. The GP3 volume type supports independent configuration of IOPS and throughput, regardless of storage capacity. GP3 costs less than io1 and io2 and can handle 15,000 IOPS, making it the most cost-effective option.

D.zh: 錯誤。io1卷類型支援高效能和獨立配置的IOPS，但成本較高。與io2類似，io1不符合「最具成本效益」的要求。
D.en: Incorrect. The io1 volume type supports high performance and independently configured IOPS, but the cost is high. Similar to io2, io1 does not meet the "most cost effective" requirement.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：C (96%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：GP3 磁碟區類型支援獨立配置 IOPS 和吞吐量，無論儲存容量為何。 GP3 的成本低於 io1 和 io2，並且可以處理 15,000 IOPS，使其成為最具成本效益的選擇。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (96%). Popular discussion and key point summary: The GP3 volume type supports independent configuration of IOPS and throughput, regardless of storage capacity. GP3 costs less than io1 and io2 and can handle 15,000 IOPS, making it the most cost-effective option.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 公司需要儲存其醫療保健應用程式中的資料。應用程式的數據經常更改。新法規要求對所有級別的儲存資料進行審計存取。該公司將應用程式託管在儲存容量不足的本地基礎設施上。解決方案架構師必須將現有資料安全地遷移到 AWS，同時滿足新法規。哪種解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company needs to store data from its healthcare application. The application’s data frequently changes. A new regulation requires audit access at all levels of the stored data. The company hosts the application on an on-premises infrastructure that is running out of storage capacity. A solutions architect must securely migrate the existing data to AWS while satisfying the new regulation. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 使用 AWS DataSync 將現有資料移至 Amazon S3。使用 AWS CloudTrail 記錄資料事件。
A.en: Use AWS DataSync to move the existing data to Amazon S3. Use AWS CloudTrail to log data events.

B.zh: 使用 AWS Snowcone 將現有資料移至 Amazon S3。使用 AWS CloudTrail 記錄管理事件。
B.en: Use AWS Snowcone to move the existing data to Amazon S3. Use AWS CloudTrail to log management events.

C.zh: 使用 Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration 將現有資料移至 Amazon S3。使用 AWS CloudTrail 記錄資料事件。
C.en: Use Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration to move the existing data to Amazon S3. Use AWS CloudTrail to log data events.

D.zh: 使用 AWS Storage Gateway 將現有資料移至 Amazon S3。使用 AWS CloudTrail 記錄管理事件。
D.en: Use AWS Storage Gateway to move the existing data to Amazon S3. Use AWS CloudTrail to log management events.

A.zh: 正確。AWS DataSync 是一項專門為有效率且安全地將資料遷移到 AWS 而設計的服務，適合處理頻繁變化的資料。 Amazon S3 是一種高度可用且高度耐用的儲存服務，適合儲存醫療現場資料。 AWS CloudTrail 可以記錄資料事件以滿足稽核需求。
A.en: Correct. AWS DataSync is a service specially designed to migrate data to AWS efficiently and securely, and is suitable for handling frequently changing data. Amazon S3 is a highly available and highly durable storage service suitable for storing medical field data. AWS CloudTrail can record data events to meet auditing needs.

B.zh: 錯誤。AWS Snowcone適合小規模資料遷移，但問題沒有提到資料的大小，Snowcone不適合頻繁變化的資料。 CloudTrail的管理事件記錄的是帳戶相關的操作，而不是資料相關的操作，無法滿足審計要求。
B.en: Incorrect. AWS Snowcone is suitable for small-scale data migration, but the question does not mention the size of the data, and Snowcone is not suitable for frequently changing data. CloudTrail's management events record account-related operations, not data-related operations, and cannot meet audit requirements.

C.zh: 錯誤。Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration適合加速跨區域資料上傳，但問題沒有提及跨區域場景。雖然 CloudTrail 可以記錄資料事件，但 Transfer Acceleration 並不是最好的遷移工具。
C.en: Incorrect. Amazon S3 Transfer Acceleration is suitable for accelerating cross-region data upload, but the question does not mention cross-region scenarios. While CloudTrail can log data events, Transfer Acceleration is not the best migration tool.

D.zh: 錯誤。AWS Storage Gateway 適用於混合雲端儲存場景，但問題需要資料遷移且不允許存取本機資料。 CloudTrail的管理事件無法滿足資料稽核需求。
D.en: Incorrect. AWS Storage Gateway is suitable for hybrid cloud storage scenarios, but the question requires data migration and does not allow local data to be accessed. CloudTrail's management events cannot meet data audit needs.

answer_text.zh: A. 使用 AWS DataSync 將現有資料移至 Amazon S3。使用 AWS CloudTrail 記錄資料事件。
answer_text.en: A. Use AWS DataSync to move the existing data to Amazon S3. Use AWS CloudTrail to log data events.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (69%)； D (31%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：AWS DataSync 是一項專門為有效率且安全地將資料遷移到 AWS 而設計的服務，適合處理頻繁變化的資料。 Amazon S3 是一種高度可用且高度耐用的儲存服務，適合儲存醫療現場資料。 AWS CloudTrail 可以記錄資料事件以滿足稽核需求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (69%); D (31%). Popular discussion and key point summary: AWS DataSync is a service specially designed to migrate data to AWS efficiently and securely, and is suitable for handling frequently changing data. Amazon S3 is a highly available and highly durable storage service suitable for storing medical field data. AWS CloudTrail can record data events to meet auditing needs.

questions.domain: 領域 1：設計彈性架構 (Design Resilient Architectures)

question_text.zh: 解決方案架構師正在使用 MySQL 資料庫實作複雜的 Java 應用程式。 Java 應用程式必須部署在 Apache Tomcat 上，並且必須具有高可用性。解決方案架構師應該怎麼做才能滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A solutions architect is implementing a complex Java application with a MySQL database. The Java application must be deployed on Apache Tomcat and must be highly available. What should the solutions architect do to meet these requirements?

A.zh: 在 AWS Lambda 中部署應用程式。設定 Amazon API Gateway API 以與 Lambda 函數連線。
A.en: Deploy the application in AWS Lambda. Configure an Amazon API Gateway API to connect with the Lambda functions.

B.zh: 使用 AWS Elastic Beanstalk 部署應用程式。配置負載平衡環境和滾動部署策略。
B.en: Deploy the application by using AWS Elastic Beanstalk. Configure a load-balanced environment and a rolling deployment policy.

C.zh: 將資料庫移轉到 Amazon ElastiCache。設定 ElastiCache 安全群組以允許從應用程式進行存取。
C.en: Migrate the database to Amazon ElastiCache. Configure the ElastiCache security group to allow access from the application.

D.zh: 啟動 Amazon EC2 執行個體。在 EC2 執行個體上安裝 MySQL 伺服器。在伺服器上配置應用程式。建立 AMI。使用 AMI 建立具有 Auto Scaling 群組的啟動範本。
D.en: Launch an Amazon EC2 instance. Install a MySQL server on the EC2 instance. Configure the application on the server. Create an AMI. Use the AMI to create a launch template with an Auto Scaling group.

A.zh: 錯誤。AWS Lambda 適用於無伺服器架構，但問題顯然需要部署複雜的 Java 應用程式。這種應用程式通常需要持久化的運行環境（例如Tomcat），而Lambda不適合運行複雜的Java應用程式。另外，Lambda無法直接滿足高可用和負載平衡的需求，連接MySQL資料庫需要額外的配置。
A.en: Incorrect. AWS Lambda is suitable for serverless architecture, but the question clearly requires the deployment of a complex Java application. This application usually requires a persistent running environment (such as Tomcat), and Lambda is not suitable for running complex Java applications. In addition, Lambda cannot directly meet the needs of high availability and load balancing, and the connection to the MySQL database requires additional configuration.

B.zh: 正確。Elastic Beanstalk 是一項託管服務，支援 Java 應用程式的部署，並且可以自動配置負載平衡和高可用性環境。捲動部署策略保證應用程式更新不會中斷服務，滿足高可用性要求。 Elastic Beanstalk也支援Tomcat作為運行環境，非常適合問題中的需求。
B.en: Correct. Elastic Beanstalk is a managed service that supports the deployment of Java applications and can automatically configure load balancing and high-availability environments. The rolling deployment strategy ensures that application updates will not interrupt service and meet high availability requirements. Elastic Beanstalk also supports Tomcat as the operating environment, which is very suitable for the needs in the question.

C.zh: 錯誤。Amazon ElastiCache 是一種記憶體快取服務，適用於加速資料庫查詢或儲存臨時數據，但無法取代 MySQL 資料庫。該問題需要MySQL資料庫，而不是快取服務，因此該選項不滿足要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Amazon ElastiCache is an in-memory caching service that is suitable for accelerating database queries or storing temporary data, but it cannot replace the MySQL database. The question requires a MySQL database, not a cache service, so this option does not meet the requirements.

D.zh: 錯誤。雖然這種方法可以實現高可用性，但需要手動配置和管理EC2實例、負載平衡器、Auto Scaling群組等，複雜度很高。 Elastic Beanstalk 可以自動處理這些任務，因此更適合問題所需的場景。
D.en: Incorrect. Although this method can achieve high availability, it requires manual configuration and management of EC2 instances, load balancers, Auto Scaling groups, etc., which is highly complex. Elastic Beanstalk can handle these tasks automatically, so it is more suitable for the scenario required by the question.

answer_text.zh: B. 使用 AWS Elastic Beanstalk 部署應用程式。配置負載平衡環境和滾動部署策略。
answer_text.en: B. Deploy the application by using AWS Elastic Beanstalk. Configure a load-balanced environment and a rolling deployment policy.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：Elastic Beanstalk 是一項託管服務，支援 Java 應用程式的部署，並且可以自動配置負載平衡和高可用性環境。捲動部署策略保證應用程式更新不會中斷服務，滿足高可用性要求。 Elastic Beanstalk也支援Tomcat作為運行環境，非常適合問題中的需求。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Elastic Beanstalk is a managed service that supports the deployment of Java applications and can automatically configure load balancing and high-availability environments. The rolling deployment strategy ensures that application updates will not interrupt service and meet high availability requirements. Elastic Beanstalk also supports Tomcat as the operating environment, which is very suitable for the needs in the question.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 無伺服器應用程式使用 Amazon API Gateway、AWS Lambda 和 Amazon DynamoDB。 Lambda 函數需要讀取和寫入 DynamoDB 表的權限。哪種解決方案可以讓 Lambda 函數最安全地存取 DynamoDB 表？
question_text.en: A serverless application uses Amazon API Gateway, AWS Lambda, and Amazon DynamoDB. The Lambda function needs permissions to read and write to the DynamoDB table. Which solution will give the Lambda function access to the DynamoDB table MOST securely?

A.zh: 建立一個能夠以程式設計方式存取 Lambda 函數的 IAM 使用者。向使用者附加允許對 DynamoDB 資料表進行讀寫存取的策略。將 access_key_id 和 Secret_access_key 參數儲存為 Lambda 環境變數的一部分。確保其他 AWS 使用者沒有對 Lambda 函數配置的讀寫存取權。
A.en: Create an IAM user with programmatic access to the Lambda function. Attach a policy to the user that allows read and write access to the DynamoDB table. Store the access_key_id and secret_access_key parameters as part of the Lambda environment variables. Ensure that other AWS users do not have read and write access to the Lambda function configuration.

B.zh: 建立一個 IAM 角色，其中包含 Lambda 作為可信任服務。將政策附加到允許對 DynamoDB 資料表進行讀寫存取的角色。更新 Lambda 函數的配置以使用新角色作為執行角色。
B.en: Create an IAM role that includes Lambda as a trusted service. Attach a policy to the role that allows read and write access to the DynamoDB table. Update the configuration of the Lambda function to use the new role as the execution role.

C.zh: 建立一個能夠以程式方式存取 Lambda 函數的 IAM 使用者。向使用者附加允許對 DynamoDB 資料表進行讀寫存取的策略。將 access_key_id 和 Secret_access_key 參數儲存在 AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store 中作為安全字串參數。更新 Lambda 函數程式碼以在連接到 DynamoDB 表之前檢索安全字串參數。
C.en: Create an IAM user with programmatic access to the Lambda function. Attach a policy to the user that allows read and write access to the DynamoDB table. Store the access_key_id and secret_access_key parameters in AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store as secure string parameters. Update the Lambda function code to retrieve the secure string parameters before connecting to the DynamoDB table.

D.zh: 建立一個 IAM 角色，其中包含 DynamoDB 作為可信任服務。將策略附加到允許從 Lambda 函數進行讀寫存取的角色。更新 Lambda 函數的程式碼以作為執行角色附加到新角色。
D.en: Create an IAM role that includes DynamoDB as a trusted service. Attach a policy to the role that allows read and write access from the Lambda function. Update the code of the Lambda function to attach to the new role as an execution role.

A.zh: 錯誤。建立具有程式存取權限的 IAM 使用者以供 Lambda 函數使用。使用 IAM 使用者並將 access_key_id 和 Secret_access_key 儲存在 Lambda 環境變數中存在安全風險。環境變數可能會意外暴露，從而違反安全最佳實踐。
A.en: Incorrect. Create an IAM user with programmatic access for use by the Lambda function. Using an IAM user and storing access_key_id and secret_access_key in Lambda environment variables is a security risk. Environment variables can be accidentally exposed, violating security best practices.

B.zh: 正確。建立一個 IAM 角色，其中包含 Lambda 作為可信任服務。這是 AWS 推薦的最佳實踐。透過將 IAM 角色指派給 Lambda 函數，Lambda 可以安全地存取 DynamoDB，而無需明確儲存憑證。
B.en: Correct. Create an IAM role that includes Lambda as a trusted service. This is an AWS recommended best practice. By assigning an IAM role to a Lambda function, Lambda can securely access DynamoDB without explicitly storing credentials.

C.zh: 錯誤。建立具有程式存取權限的 IAM 使用者以供 Lambda 函數使用。儘管使用 AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store 儲存憑證比直接將憑證儲存在環境變數中更安全，但它仍然不如使用 IAM 角色安全。 IAM 使用者的憑證可能會受到洩漏。
C.en: Incorrect. Create an IAM user with programmatic access for use by the Lambda function. Although using AWS Systems Manager Parameter Store to store credentials is more secure than storing them directly in environment variables, it is still not as secure as using IAM roles. An IAM user's credentials may be compromised.

D.zh: 錯誤。建立一個 IAM 角色，其中包含 DynamoDB 作為可信任服務。 IAM 角色的信任關係應該是 Lambda，而不是 DynamoDB。 DynamoDB 不需要成為可信任服務。
D.en: Incorrect. Create an IAM role that includes DynamoDB as a trusted service. The IAM role's trust relationship should be Lambda, not DynamoDB. DynamoDB does not need to be a trusted service.

answer_text.zh: B. 建立一個 IAM 角色，其中包含 Lambda 作為可信任服務。將政策附加到允許對 DynamoDB 資料表進行讀寫存取的角色。更新 Lambda 函數的配置以使用新角色作為執行角色。
answer_text.en: B. Create an IAM role that includes Lambda as a trusted service. Attach a policy to the role that allows read and write access to the DynamoDB table. Update the configuration of the Lambda function to use the new role as the execution role.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：建立一個 IAM 角色，其中包含 Lambda 作為可信任服務。這是 AWS 推薦的最佳實踐。透過將 IAM 角色指派給 Lambda 函數，Lambda 可以安全地存取 DynamoDB，而無需明確儲存憑證。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Create an IAM role that includes Lambda as a trusted service. This is an AWS recommended best practice. By assigning an IAM role to a Lambda function, Lambda can securely access DynamoDB without explicitly storing credentials.

questions.domain: 領域 3：設計安全架構 (Design Secure Architectures)

question_text.zh: 以下 IAM 策略附加到 IAM 群組。這是應用於該組的唯一策略。該策略對群組成員的有效 IAM 權限有哪些？
question_text.en: The following IAM policy is attached to an IAM group. This is the only policy applied to the group. What are the effective IAM permissions of this policy for group members?

A.zh: 允許群組成員在 us-east-1 區域內執行任何 Amazon EC2 操作。不應用「允許」權限之後的語句。
A.en: Group members are permitted any Amazon EC2 action within the us-east-1 Region. Statements after the Allow permission are not applied.

B.zh: 群組成員在 us-east-1 區域中被拒絕任何 Amazon EC2 權限，除非他們使用多重驗證 (MFA) 登入。
B.en: Group members are denied any Amazon EC2 permissions in the us-east-1 Region unless they are logged in with multi-factor authentication (MFA).

C.zh: 使用多重驗證 (MFA) 登入時，群組成員可以擁有所有區域的 ec2:StopInstances 和 ec2:TerminateInstances 權限。允許群組成員執行任何其他 Amazon EC2 操作。
C.en: Group members are allowed the ec2:StopInstances and ec2:TerminateInstances permissions for all Regions when logged in with multi-factor authentication (MFA). Group members are permitted any other Amazon EC2 action.

D.zh: 只有在使用多重驗證 (MFA) 登入時，群組成員才可以擁有 us-east-1 區域的 ec2:StopInstances 和 ec2:TerminateInstances 權限。允許群組成員在 us-east-1 區域內執行任何其他 Amazon EC2 操作。
D.en: Group members are allowed the ec2:StopInstances and ec2:TerminateInstances permissions for the us-east-1 Region only when logged in with multi-factor authentication (MFA). Group members are permitted any other Amazon EC2 action within the us-east-1 Region.

A.zh: 錯誤。IAM 策略的評估順序是所有語句都會被評估，並且拒絕權限優先於允許權限。因此，後續的deny語句會影響最終的權限。
A.en: Incorrect. The evaluation order of IAM policies is that all statements will be evaluated, and Deny permissions take precedence over Allow permissions. Therefore, subsequent deny statements affect the final permissions.

B.zh: 錯誤。策略中的 us-east-1 區域內明確允許所有 EC2 操作 (ec2:*)，當不使用 MFA 時，僅拒絕特定操作（ec2:StopInstances 和 ec2:TerminateInstances）。
B.en: Incorrect. All EC2 operations (ec2:*) are explicitly allowed within the us-east-1 region in the policy, only specific operations (ec2:StopInstances and ec2:TerminateInstances) are denied when MFA is not used.

C.zh: 錯誤。策略中的allow語句明確將操作範圍限制在us-east-1區域，因此其他區域的權限不適用。
C.en: Incorrect. The allow statement in the policy explicitly limits the scope of the operation to the us-east-1 region, so permissions from other regions do not apply.

D.zh: 正確。該策略的第一個聲明允許所有 EC2 操作在 us-east-1 區域內執行。第二條語句拒絕 ec2:StopInstances 和 ec2:TerminateInstances 操作，除非使用者使用 MFA。所以最終的權威符合描述。
D.en: Correct. The first statement of the policy allows all EC2 operations to be performed within the us-east-1 region. The second statement denies the ec2:StopInstances and ec2:TerminateInstances operations unless the user uses MFA. So the final authority fits the description.

answer_text.zh: D. 只有在使用多重驗證 (MFA) 登入時，群組成員才可以擁有 us-east-1 區域的 ec2:StopInstances 和 ec2:TerminateInstances 權限。允許群組成員在 us-east-1 區域內執行任何其他 Amazon EC2 操作。
answer_text.en: D. Group members are allowed the ec2:StopInstances and ec2:TerminateInstances permissions for the us-east-1 Region only when logged in with multi-factor authentication (MFA). Group members are permitted any other Amazon EC2 action within the us-east-1 Region.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：D (90%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：該策略的第一個聲明允許所有 EC2 操作在 us-east-1 區域內執行。第二條語句拒絕 ec2:StopInstances 和 ec2:TerminateInstances 操作，除非使用者使用 MFA。所以最終的權威符合描述。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: D (90%). Popular discussion and key point summary: The first statement of the policy allows all EC2 operations to be performed within the us-east-1 region. The second statement denies the ec2:StopInstances and ec2:TerminateInstances operations unless the user uses MFA. So the final authority fits the description.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家製造公司擁有將 .csv 檔案上傳到 Amazon S3 儲存桶的機器感測器。這些 .csv 檔案必須轉換為影像，並且必須盡快提供以自動產生圖形報告。影像在 1 個月後變得無關緊要，但必須保留 .csv 文件，以便每年訓練兩次機器學習 (ML) 模型。機器學習訓練和審核是提前幾週規劃的。哪一種步驟組合能夠最經濟有效地滿足這些要求？ （選兩個。）
question_text.en: A manufacturing company has machine sensors that upload .csv files to an Amazon S3 bucket. These .csv files must be converted into images and must be made available as soon as possible for the automatic generation of graphical reports. The images become irrelevant after 1 month, but the .csv files must be kept to train machine learning (ML) models twice a year. The ML trainings and audits are planned weeks in advance. Which combination of steps will meet these requirements MOST cost-effectively? (Choose two.)

A.zh: 啟動 Amazon EC2 Spot 執行個體，每小時下載 .csv 檔案、產生映像檔並將映像上傳至 S3 儲存桶。
A.en: Launch an Amazon EC2 Spot Instance that downloads the .csv files every hour, generates the image files, and uploads the images to the S3 bucket.

B.zh: 設計一個 AWS Lambda 函數，將 .csv 檔案轉換為映像並將映像儲存在 S3 儲存桶中。上傳 .csv 檔案時呼叫 Lambda 函數。
B.en: Design an AWS Lambda function that converts the .csv files into images and stores the images in the S3 bucket. Invoke the Lambda function when a .csv file is uploaded.

C.zh: 為 S3 儲存桶中的 .csv 檔案和映像檔建立 S3 生命週期規則。上傳 1 天后，將 .csv 檔案從 S3 Standard 轉換到 S3 Glacier。圖像檔案將在 30 天後過期。
C.en: Create S3 Lifecycle rules for .csv files and image files in the S3 bucket. Transition the .csv files from S3 Standard to S3 Glacier 1 day after they are uploaded. Expire the image files after 30 days.

D.zh: 為 S3 儲存桶中的 .csv 檔案和映像檔建立 S3 生命週期規則。上傳 1 天后，將 .csv 檔案從 S3 標準轉換為 S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access (S3 One Zone-IA)。圖像檔案將在 30 天後過期。
D.en: Create S3 Lifecycle rules for .csv files and image files in the S3 bucket. Transition the .csv files from S3 Standard to S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access (S3 One Zone-IA) 1 day after they are uploaded. Expire the image files after 30 days.

E.zh: 為 S3 儲存桶中的 .csv 檔案和映像檔建立 S3 生命週期規則。上傳 1 天后，將 .csv 檔案從 S3 標準轉換為 S3 標準-不頻繁存取 (S3 標準-IA)。將影像檔案保存在低冗餘儲存 (RRS) 中。
E.en: Create S3 Lifecycle rules for .csv files and image files in the S3 bucket. Transition the .csv files from S3 Standard to S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA) 1 day after they are uploaded. Keep the image files in Reduced Redundancy Storage (RRS).

A.zh: 錯誤。此選項EC2 Spot 執行個體雖然價格便宜，但需要手動管理執行個體的生命週期，無法保證執行個體的持續可用性。相較之下，AWS Lambda更適合事件驅動的任務處理。
A.en: Incorrect. Launch an Amazon EC2 Spot Instance that downloads .csv files every hour, generates image files, and uploads the images to an S3 bucket. Although EC2 Spot Instance is cheaper, it requires manual management of the life cycle of the instance and cannot guarantee the continuous availability of the instance. In comparison, AWS Lambda is more suitable for event-driven task processing.

B.zh: 正確。設計一個 AWS Lambda 函數，將 .csv 檔案轉換為映像並將映像儲存在 S3 儲存桶中。上傳 .csv 檔案時會呼叫 Lambda 函數。 AWS Lambda 是一種無伺服器運算服務，支援事件驅動架構，可在檔案上傳到 S3 時自動觸發處理任務。 Lambda的費用是按照使用量計費的，非常適合這種場景。
B.en: Correct. Design an AWS Lambda function to convert a .csv file into an image and store the image in an S3 bucket. The Lambda function is called when the .csv file is uploaded. AWS Lambda is a serverless computing service that supports event-driven architecture and can automatically trigger processing tasks when files are uploaded to S3. Lambda's cost is billed based on usage, which is very suitable for this scenario.

C.zh: 正確。為 S3 儲存桶中的 .csv 檔案和映像檔建立 S3 生命週期規則。將 .csv 檔案從 S3 Standard 移至 S3 Glacier，並在上傳 1 天後轉換。圖像檔在 30 天後過期。 S3生命週期規則可以自動管理文件的儲存類型和過期時間。將 .csv 檔案移至 S3 Glacier 可以顯著降低儲存成本，且影像檔案會在 30 天後過期。
C.en: Correct. Create S3 lifecycle rules for .csv files and image files in S3 buckets. Move .csv files from S3 Standard to S3 Glacier, converting 1 day after upload. Expire image files after 30 days. S3 Lifecycle rules can automatically manage the storage type and expiration time of files. Moving .csv files to S3 Glacier can significantly reduce storage costs, and image files expire after 30 days.

D.zh: 錯誤。為 S3 儲存桶中的 .csv 檔案和映像檔建立 S3 生命週期規則。將 .csv 檔案從 S3 Standard 移至 S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access (S3 One Zone-IA)，並在上傳 1 天後轉換。圖像檔在 30 天後過期。 S3 One Zone-IA雖然成本較低，但可用性不高。資料儲存在單一可用區中，這會帶來資料遺失的風險。相比之下，S3 Glacier更適合長期存儲，成本也更低。
D.en: Incorrect. Create S3 lifecycle rules for .csv files and image files in S3 buckets. Move .csv files from S3 Standard to S3 One Zone-Infrequent Access (S3 One Zone-IA), converting 1 day after upload. Expire image files after 30 days. Although S3 One Zone-IA is lower in cost, it does not have high availability. Data is stored in a single availability zone, which poses the risk of data loss. In comparison, S3 Glacier is more suitable for long-term storage and has lower cost.

E.zh: 錯誤。為 S3 儲存桶中的 .csv 檔案和映像檔建立 S3 生命週期規則。將 .csv 檔案從 S3 Standard 移至 S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA)，並在上傳 1 天後轉換。將影像檔案保存在低冗餘儲存 (RRS) 中。 AWS 已棄用減少冗餘儲存 (RRS)，因此不建議使用。 S3 Standard-IA雖然適合不常存取的數據，但其成本高於S3 Glacier，不符合成本優化的要求。
E.en: Incorrect. Create S3 lifecycle rules for .csv files and image files in S3 buckets. Move .csv files from S3 Standard to S3 Standard-Infrequent Access (S3 Standard-IA), converting 1 day after upload. Keep image files in Reduced Redundancy Storage (RRS). Reduced Redundancy Storage (RRS) has been deprecated by AWS and is not recommended. Although S3 Standard-IA is suitable for infrequently accessed data, its cost is higher than S3 Glacier, which does not meet the requirements for cost optimization.

answer_text.zh: B. 設計一個 AWS Lambda 函數，將 .csv 檔案轉換為映像並將映像儲存在 S3 儲存桶中。上傳 .csv 檔案時呼叫 Lambda 函數。；C. 為 S3 儲存桶中的 .csv 檔案和映像檔建立 S3 生命週期規則。上傳 1 天后，將 .csv 檔案從 S3 Standard 轉換到 S3 Glacier。圖像檔案將在 30 天後過期。
answer_text.en: B. Design an AWS Lambda function that converts the .csv files into images and stores the images in the S3 bucket. Invoke the Lambda function when a .csv file is uploaded.; C. Create S3 Lifecycle rules for .csv files and image files in the S3 bucket. Transition the .csv files from S3 Standard to S3 Glacier 1 day after they are uploaded. Expire the image files after 30 days.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：BC (90%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：設計一個 AWS Lambda 函數，將 .csv 檔案轉換為映像並將映像儲存在 S3 儲存桶中。上傳 .csv 檔案時會呼叫 Lambda 函數。 AWS Lambda 是一種無伺服器運算服務，支援事件驅動架構，可在檔案上傳到 S3 時自動觸發處理任務。 Lambda的費用是按照使用量計費的，非常適合這種場景。；為 S3 儲存桶中的 .csv 檔案和映像檔建立 S3 生命週期規則。將 .csv 檔案從 S3 Standard 移至 S3 Glacier，並在上傳 1 天後轉換。圖像檔在 30 天後過期。 S3生命週期規則可以自動管理文件的儲存類型和過期時間。將 .csv 檔案移至 S3 Glacier 可以顯著降低儲存成本，且影像檔案會在 30 天後過期。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: BC (90%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Design an AWS Lambda function to convert a .csv file into an image and store the image in an S3 bucket. The Lambda function is called when the .csv file is uploaded. AWS Lambda is a serverless computing service that supports event-driven architecture and can automatically trigger processing tasks when files are uploaded to S3. Lambda's cost is billed based on usage, which is very suitable for this scenario.; Create S3 lifecycle rules for .csv files and image files in S3 buckets. Move .csv files from S3 Standard to S3 Glacier, converting 1 day after upload. Expire image files after 30 days. S3 Lifecycle rules can automatically manage the storage type and expiration time of files. Moving .csv files to S3 Glacier can significantly reduce storage costs, and image files expire after 30 days.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司開發了一款新的視訊遊戲作為網路應用程式。該應用程式採用 VPC 中的三層架構，資料庫層為 Amazon RDS for MySQL。多名選手將同時在線上比賽。遊戲的開發人員希望近乎即時地顯示前 10 名記分牌，並提供停止和恢復遊戲的能力，同時保留當前分數。解決方案架構師應該怎麼做才能滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company has developed a new video game as a web application. The application is in a three-tier architecture in a VPC with Amazon RDS for MySQL in the database layer. Several players will compete concurrently online. The game’s developers want to display a top-10 scoreboard in near-real time and offer the ability to stop and restore the game while preserving the current scores. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements?

A.zh: 設定 Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached 叢集來快取要顯示的 Web 應用程式的分數。
A.en: Set up an Amazon ElastiCache for Memcached cluster to cache the scores for the web application to display.

B.zh: 設定 Amazon ElastiCache for Redis 叢集來計算和快取要顯示的 Web 應用程式的分數。
B.en: Set up an Amazon ElastiCache for Redis cluster to compute and cache the scores for the web application to display.

C.zh: 將 Amazon CloudFront 指派放置在 Web 應用程式前面，以將記分板快取在應用程式的一部分中。
C.en: Place an Amazon CloudFront distribution in front of the web application to cache the scoreboard in a section of the application.

D.zh: 在 Amazon RDS for MySQL 上建立唯讀副本以執行查詢來計算記分板並向 Web 應用程式提供讀取流量。
D.en: Create a read replica on Amazon RDS for MySQL to run queries to compute the scoreboard and serve the read traffic to the web application.

A.zh: 錯誤。Memcached是一個高效能的分散式記憶體物件快取系統，適用於簡單的key-value儲存和快取場景。然而，該問題需要「計算和快取分數」。 Memcached不支援複雜的資料結構和計算功能，因此不太適合這種場景。 Memcached無法滿足運算需求。
A.en: Incorrect. Memcached is a high-performance distributed memory object caching system suitable for simple key-value storage and caching scenarios. However, the question requires "calculating and caching scores". Memcached does not support complex data structures and calculation functions, so it is not suitable for this scenario. Memcached cannot meet the computing needs.

B.zh: 正確。Redis支援豐富的資料結構（如Sorted Sets），可以輕鬆實現排序功能。 Redis還支援持久化功能，可以在遊戲停止和恢復時保留當前分數。 Redis完全滿足題目要求，包括運算、快取和持久化功能。
B.en: Correct. Redis supports rich data structures (such as Sorted Sets), which can easily implement the ranking function. Redis also supports persistence capabilities, which can retain the current score when the game is stopped and resumed. Redis fully meets the question requirements, including calculation, caching and persistence functions.

C.zh: 錯誤。CloudFront 是一個內容傳遞網路 (CDN)，主要用於快取靜態內容以提高全域存取速度。這個問題需要「計算並快取分數」而不是靜態內容的分發，因此CloudFront不適合這種場景。 CloudFront 無法處理動態運算需求。
C.en: Incorrect. CloudFront is a content delivery network (CDN) mainly used to cache static content to improve global access speeds. The question requires "calculating and caching scores" rather than the distribution of static content, so CloudFront is not suitable for this scenario. CloudFront cannot handle dynamic computing requirements.

D.zh: 錯誤。RDS只讀副本可以分擔讀取壓力，但仍需要SQL查詢來計算排名，效率較低。題目要求「近實時」顯示排名，使用資料庫查詢可能無法滿足效能要求。資料庫查詢效率低，無法滿足即時性要求。
D.en: Incorrect. RDS read replicas can share the read pressure, but it still requires SQL queries to calculate the rankings, which is less efficient. The question requires "near real-time" display of rankings, and using database queries may not meet performance requirements. The database query efficiency is low and cannot meet real-time requirements.

answer_text.zh: B. 設定 Amazon ElastiCache for Redis 叢集來計算和快取要顯示的 Web 應用程式的分數。
answer_text.en: B. Set up an Amazon ElastiCache for Redis cluster to compute and cache the scores for the web application to display.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (92%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：Redis支援豐富的資料結構（如Sorted Sets），可以輕鬆實現排序功能。 Redis還支援持久化功能，可以在遊戲停止和恢復時保留當前分數。 Redis完全滿足題目要求，包括運算、快取和持久化功能。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (92%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Redis supports rich data structures (such as Sorted Sets), which can easily implement the ranking function. Redis also supports persistence capabilities, which can retain the current score when the game is stopped and resumed. Redis fully meets the question requirements, including calculation, caching and persistence functions.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家電子商務公司希望使用機器學習 (ML) 演算法來建立和訓練模型。該公司將使用這些模型來視覺化複雜的場景並檢測客戶資料的趨勢。架構團隊希望將其 ML 模型與報告平臺集成，以分析增強數據並直接在其商業智慧儀錶板中使用數據。哪種解決方案能夠以最少的營運開銷滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: An ecommerce company wants to use machine learning (ML) algorithms to build and train models. The company will use the models to visualize complex scenarios and to detect trends in customer data. The architecture team wants to integrate its ML models with a reporting platform to analyze the augmented data and use the data directly in its business intelligence dashboards. Which solution will meet these requirements with the LEAST operational overhead?

A.zh: 使用 AWS Glue 建立 ML 轉換來建立和訓練模型。使用 Amazon OpenSearch Service 視覺化資料。
A.en: Use AWS Glue to create an ML transform to build and train models. Use Amazon OpenSearch Service to visualize the data.

B.zh: 使用 Amazon SageMaker 建立和訓練模型。使用 Amazon QuickSight 視覺化資料。
B.en: Use Amazon SageMaker to build and train models. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the data.

C.zh: 使用 AWS Marketplace 中預先建置的 ML Amazon 系統映像 (AMI) 來建置和訓練模型。使用 Amazon OpenSearch Service 視覺化資料。
C.en: Use a pre-built ML Amazon Machine Image (AMI) from the AWS Marketplace to build and train models. Use Amazon OpenSearch Service to visualize the data.

D.zh: 使用 Amazon QuickSight 透過計算欄位來建立和訓練模型。使用 Amazon QuickSight 視覺化資料。
D.en: Use Amazon QuickSight to build and train models by using calculated fields. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the data.

A.zh: 錯誤。此選項錯誤：AWS Glue 主要用於 ETL 任務，並不是專門為建造和訓練機器學習模型而設計的。雖然AWS Glue具有ML轉換功能，但其用途有限，無法滿足複雜的機器學習需求。錯誤：Amazon OpenSearch Service 主要用於搜尋和分析數據，並不是專門為商業智慧視覺化而設計的。
A.en: Incorrect. Use AWS Glue to create an ML transformation to build and train models. Use Amazon OpenSearch Service to visualize data. Wrong: AWS Glue is primarily used for ETL tasks and is not specifically designed for building and training machine learning models. Although AWS Glue has ML conversion capabilities, its uses are limited and cannot meet complex machine learning needs. Wrong: Amazon OpenSearch Service is primarily used for searching and analyzing data and is not specifically designed for business intelligence visualization.

B.zh: 正確。此選項Amazon SageMaker 是 AWS 專門設計的機器學習服務，可有效建置、訓練和部署模型，以滿足複雜的機器學習需求。 Amazon QuickSight是AWS的商業智慧工具，可直接整合資料並進行視覺化分析，適合在商業智慧儀錶板中使用。
B.en: Correct. Build and train models using Amazon SageMaker. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize data. Amazon SageMaker is a machine learning service specially designed by AWS that can efficiently build, train, and deploy models to meet complex machine learning needs. Amazon QuickSight is a business intelligence tool from AWS that can directly integrate data and perform visual analysis, and is suitable for use in business intelligence dashboards.

C.zh: 錯誤。使用 AWS Marketplace 中預先建置的 ML Amazon 系統映像 (AMI) 建置和訓練模型。使用 Amazon OpenSearch Service 視覺化資料。 AWS Marketplace預先建立的AMI雖然可以快速部署機器學習環境，但需要使用者主動管理和維護，增加了營運開銷。 Amazon OpenSearch Service 不適合商業智慧視覺化。
C.en: Incorrect. Build and train models using pre-built ML Amazon Machine Images (AMIs) from the AWS Marketplace. Use Amazon OpenSearch Service to visualize data. Although the pre-built AMI of AWS Marketplace can quickly deploy a machine learning environment, it requires user active management and maintenance, which increases operational overhead. Amazon OpenSearch Service is not suitable for business intelligence visualization.

D.zh: 錯誤。使用 Amazon QuickSight 建立和訓練具有計算欄位的模型。使用 Amazon QuickSight 視覺化資料。 Amazon QuickSight 是一種資料視覺化工具，不適合建立和訓練機器單字學習模型。 Amazon QuickSight 可用於資料視覺化，但無法執行機器學習任務。
D.en: Incorrect. Use Amazon QuickSight to build and train models with calculated fields. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize data. Amazon QuickSight is a data visualization tool and is not suitable for building and training machine word learning models. Amazon QuickSight can be used for data visualization, but it cannot perform machine learning tasks.

answer_text.zh: B. 使用 Amazon SageMaker 建立和訓練模型。使用 Amazon QuickSight 視覺化資料。
answer_text.en: B. Use Amazon SageMaker to build and train models. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize the data.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：B (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：此選項Amazon SageMaker 是 AWS 專門設計的機器學習服務，可有效建置、訓練和部署模型，以滿足複雜的機器學習需求。 Amazon QuickSight是AWS的商業智慧工具，可直接整合資料並進行視覺化分析，適合在商業智慧儀錶板中使用。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: B (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Build and train models using Amazon SageMaker. Use Amazon QuickSight to visualize data. Amazon SageMaker is a machine learning service specially designed by AWS that can efficiently build, train, and deploy models to meet complex machine learning needs. Amazon QuickSight is a business intelligence tool from AWS that can directly integrate data and perform visual analysis, and is suitable for use in business intelligence dashboards.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司正在多個 AWS 帳戶中執行其生產和非生產環境工作負載。這些帳戶位於 AWS Organizations 中的組織中。該公司需要設計一種解決方案來防止成本使用標籤被修改。哪種解決方案可以滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company is running its production and nonproduction environment workloads in multiple AWS accounts. The accounts are in an organization in AWS Organizations. The company needs to design a solution that will prevent the modification of cost usage tags. Which solution will meet these requirements?

A.zh: 建立自訂 AWS Config 規則以防止授權主體以外的標籤修改。
A.en: Create a custom AWS Config rule to prevent tag modification except by authorized principals.

B.zh: 在 AWS CloudTrail 中建立自訂追蹤以防止標籤修改。
B.en: Create a custom trail in AWS CloudTrail to prevent tag modification.

C.zh: 建立服務控制策略 (SCP) 以防止授權主體以外的標籤修改。
C.en: Create a service control policy (SCP) to prevent tag modification except by authorized principals.

D.zh: 建立自訂 Amazon CloudWatch 日誌以防止標籤被修改。
D.en: Create custom Amazon CloudWatch logs to prevent tag modification.

A.zh: 錯誤。AWS Config 主要用於資源合規性檢查和記錄資源配置更改，但不能直接阻止操作（例如標籤修改）。它可以檢測違規行為，但無法主動阻止修改。
A.en: Incorrect. AWS Config is mainly used for resource compliance checking and recording resource configuration changes, but it cannot directly block operations (such as label modifications). It can detect violations but cannot proactively prevent modifications.

B.zh: 錯誤。AWS CloudTrail 用於記錄 API 呼叫和活動日誌以協助審核和控制，但它不能直接阻止操作（例如標籤修改）。
B.en: Incorrect. AWS CloudTrail is used to record API calls and activity logs to help with auditing and control, but it cannot directly block operations (such as label modifications).

C.zh: 正確。SCP 是 AWS Organizations 中的一項政策，可以限製或允許群組中帳戶的操作。透過SCP，可以明確限制標記修改操作，以確保只有授權主體才能執行此操作。
C.en: Correct. SCP is a policy in AWS Organizations that can limit or allow the operations of accounts in a group. Through SCP, mark modification operations can be explicitly restricted to ensure that only authorized subjects can perform this operation.

D.zh: 錯誤。CloudWatch Logs 用於控制和記錄日誌數據，但無法處理阻塞操作（例如標籤修改）。
D.en: Incorrect. CloudWatch Logs is used to control and record log data, but it cannot handle blocking operations (such as label modification).

answer_text.zh: C. 建立服務控制策略 (SCP) 以防止授權主體以外的標籤修改。
answer_text.en: C. Create a service control policy (SCP) to prevent tag modification except by authorized principals.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：C (100%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：SCP 是 AWS Organizations 中的一項政策，可以限製或允許群組中帳戶的操作。透過SCP，可以明確限制標記修改操作，以確保只有授權主體才能執行此操作。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: C (100%). Popular discussion and key point summary: SCP is a policy in AWS Organizations that can limit or allow the operations of accounts in a group. Through SCP, mark modification operations can be explicitly restricted to ensure that only authorized subjects can perform this operation.

questions.domain: 領域 2：設計高性能架構 (Design High-Performing Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司在 AWS 雲端中託管其應用程式。該應用程式在 Auto Scaling 群組中的 Elastic Load Balancer 後面的 Amazon EC2 執行個體上運行，並使用 Amazon DynamoDB 表。該公司希望確保該應用程式可以在另一個 AWS 區域中使用，並且停機時間最短。解決方案架構師應該如何做才能以最少的停機時間來滿足這些要求？
question_text.en: A company hosts its application in the AWS Cloud. The application runs on Amazon EC2 instances behind an Elastic Load Balancer in an Auto Scaling group and with an Amazon DynamoDB table. The company wants to ensure the application can be made available in anotherAWS Region with minimal downtime. What should a solutions architect do to meet these requirements with the LEAST amount of downtime?

A.zh: 在災難復原區域中建立 Auto Scaling 群組和負載平衡器。將 DynamoDB 表配置為全域表。配置 DNS 故障轉移以指向新的災難復原區域的負載平衡器。
A.en: Create an Auto Scaling group and a load balancer in the disaster recovery Region. Configure the DynamoDB table as a global table. Configure DNS failover to point to the new disaster recovery Region's load balancer.

B.zh: 建立 AWS CloudFormation 範本來建立 EC2 執行個體、負載平衡器和 DynamoDB 表，以便在需要時啟動 設定 DNS 故障轉移以指向新的災難復原區域的負載平衡器。
B.en: Create an AWS CloudFormation template to create EC2 instances, load balancers, and DynamoDB tables to be launched when needed Configure DNS failover to point to the new disaster recovery Region's load balancer.

C.zh: 建立 AWS CloudFormation 範本以建立 EC2 執行個體和需要時啟動的負載平衡器。將 DynamoDB 表配置為全域表。配置 DNS 故障轉移以指向新的災難復原區域的負載平衡器。
C.en: Create an AWS CloudFormation template to create EC2 instances and a load balancer to be launched when needed. Configure the DynamoDB table as a global table. Configure DNS failover to point to the new disaster recovery Region's load balancer.

D.zh: 在災難復原區域中建立 Auto Scaling 群組和負載平衡器。將 DynamoDB 表配置為全域表。建立 Amazon CloudWatch 警報以觸發 AWS Lambda 函數，該函數更新指向災難復原負載平衡器的 Amazon Route 53。
D.en: Create an Auto Scaling group and load balancer in the disaster recovery Region. Configure the DynamoDB table as a global table. Create an Amazon CloudWatch alarm to trigger an AWS Lambda function that updates Amazon Route 53 pointing to the disaster recovery load balancer.

A.zh: 正確。在災備區域建立Auto Scaling群組和負載平衡器可以確保應用程式能夠在該區域快速啟動和運作。將DynamoDB表配置為全域表，可實現跨區域資料同步，確保資料一致性。設定 DNS 故障轉移可讓您在主區域不可用時快速切換到災難復原區域。此選項滿足問題要求並提供最短的停機時間。
A.en: Correct. Creating Auto Scaling groups and load balancers in the disaster recovery Region can ensure that applications can start and run quickly in this area. Configuring DynamoDB tables as global tables can achieve cross-regional data synchronization and ensure data consistency. Configuring DNS failover allows you to quickly switch to the disaster recovery Region if the primary zone becomes unavailable. This option meets the question requirements and provides minimal downtime.

B.zh: 錯誤。使用CloudFormation範本可以快速部署資源，但災難發生時需要手動啟動資源，可能會導致長時間停機。如果不事先配置DynamoDB全域表，可能會出現資料同步問題。此選項不符合最短停機時間要求。
B.en: Incorrect. Using CloudFormation templates can quickly deploy resources, but resources need to be manually started when a disaster occurs, which may result in long downtime. If DynamoDB global tables are not configured in advance, data synchronization problems may occur. This option does not meet the Minimum Downtime requirement.

C.zh: 錯誤。雖然配置了 DynamoDB 全域表，但在災難發生時使用 CloudFormation 範本啟動資源仍然會導致長時間停機。此選項不符合最短停機時間要求。
C.en: Incorrect. Although DynamoDB global tables are configured, using CloudFormation templates to start resources when a disaster occurs still results in long downtime. This option does not meet the Minimum Downtime requirement.

D.zh: 錯誤。建立 Auto Scaling 群組和負載平衡器以及設定 DynamoDB 全域表是正確的步驟。使用 CloudWatch 和 Lambda 函數更新 Route 53 的 DNS 記錄是一種自動化方法，但比直接配置 DNS 故障轉移更複雜，並且可能會增加延遲。此選項不符合最短停機時間要求。
D.en: Incorrect. Creating the Auto Scaling group and load balancer and configuring DynamoDB global tables are the correct steps. Using CloudWatch and Lambda functions to update Route 53's DNS records is an automated approach, but is more complex than directly configuring DNS failover and may increase latency. This option does not meet the Minimum Downtime requirement.

answer_text.zh: A. 在災難復原區域中建立 Auto Scaling 群組和負載平衡器。將 DynamoDB 表配置為全域表。配置 DNS 故障轉移以指向新的災難復原區域的負載平衡器。
answer_text.en: A. Create an Auto Scaling group and a load balancer in the disaster recovery Region. Configure the DynamoDB table as a global table. Configure DNS failover to point to the new disaster recovery Region's load balancer.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (57%)； C (38%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：在災備區域建立Auto Scaling群組和負載平衡器可以確保應用程式能夠在該區域快速啟動和運作。將DynamoDB表配置為全域表，可實現跨區域資料同步，確保資料一致性。設定 DNS 故障轉移可讓您在主區域不可用時快速切換到災難復原區域。此選項滿足問題要求並提供最短的停機時間。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (57%); C (38%). Popular discussion and key point summary: Creating Auto Scaling groups and load balancers in the disaster recovery Region can ensure that applications can start and run quickly in this area. Configuring DynamoDB tables as global tables can achieve cross-regional data synchronization and ensure data consistency. Configuring DNS failover allows you to quickly switch to the disaster recovery Region if the primary zone becomes unavailable. This option meets the question requirements and provides minimal downtime.

questions.domain: 領域 4：設計成本優化架構 (Design Cost-Optimized Architectures)

question_text.zh: 一家公司需要在 2 週內將 MySQL 資料庫從其本地資料中心遷移到 AWS。該資料庫大小為 20 TB。該公司希望以最短的停機時間完成遷移。哪一種解決方案能夠最經濟有效地遷移資料庫？
question_text.en: A company needs to migrate a MySQL database from its on-premises data center to AWS within 2 weeks. The database is 20 TB in size. The company wants to complete the migration with minimal downtime. Which solution will migrate the database MOST cost-effectively?

A.zh: 訂購 AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized 設備。將 AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) 與 AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) 結合使用來遷移資料庫並複製持續變更。將 Snowball Edge 裝置傳送到 AWS 以完成遷移並繼續正在進行的複製。
A.en: Order an AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized device. Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) with AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) to migrate the database with replication of ongoing changes. Send the Snowball Edge device to AWS to finish the migration and continue the ongoing replication.

B.zh: 訂購 AWS Snowmobile 車輛。將 AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) 與 AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) 結合使用來遷移正在進行變更的資料庫。將 Snowmobile 車輛發送回 AWS 以完成遷移並繼續正在進行的複製。
B.en: Order an AWS Snowmobile vehicle. Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) with AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) to migrate the database with ongoing changes. Send the Snowmobile vehicle back to AWS to finish the migration and continue the ongoing replication.

C.zh: 訂購 AWS Snowball Edge Compute Optimized with GPU 裝置。將 AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) 與 AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) 結合使用來遷移正在進行變更的資料庫。將 Snowball 裝置傳送到 AWS 以完成遷移並繼續正在進行的複製
C.en: Order an AWS Snowball Edge Compute Optimized with GPU device. Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) with AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) to migrate the database with ongoing changes. Send the Snowball device to AWS to finish the migration and continue the ongoing replication

D.zh: 訂購 1 GB 專用 AWS Direct Connect 連線以與資料中心建立連線。將 AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) 與 AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) 結合使用來遷移資料庫並複製持續變更。
D.en: Order a 1 GB dedicated AWS Direct Connect connection to establish a connection with the data center. Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) with AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) to migrate the database with replication of ongoing changes.

A.zh: 正確。此選項使用 AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized 裝置進行遷移，結合 AWS DMS 和 AWS SCT 進行資料庫遷移和連續複製。 Snowball Edge Storage Optimized 專為大規模資料遷移而設計，支援以低成本高效傳輸 20 TB 資料。此方法適用於需要快速遷移且網路頻寬有限的場景。
A.en: Correct. This option uses an AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized device for migration, combining AWS DMS and AWS SCT for database migration and continuous replication. Snowball Edge Storage Optimized is designed for large-scale data migration, supporting the efficient transfer of 20 TB of data at a low cost. This method is suitable for scenarios where rapid migration is required and network bandwidth is limited.

B.zh: 錯誤。AWS Snowmobile 是一種用於遷移 PB 級資料的解決方案，適用於超大規模資料遷移。 20TB的資料量遠小於Snowmobile的設計容量。使用雪地摩託車會導致資源浪費和成本高昂。
B.en: Incorrect. AWS Snowmobile is a solution for migrating petabytes of data and is suitable for very large-scale data migrations. The data volume of 20 TB is far less than the design capacity of Snowmobile. Using Snowmobile will lead to a waste of resources and high costs.

C.zh: 錯誤。Snowball Edge 運算最佳化裝置主要用於需要運算能力的場景（例如機器學習或影像處理），而不是為資料遷移而設計的。此選項不滿足問題的要求，且成本基於儲存最佳化設備。
C.en: Incorrect. Snowball Edge Compute Optimized devices are mainly used for scenarios that require computing power (such as machine learning or image processing) and are not designed for data migration. This option does not meet the requirements of the question and the cost is based on Storage Optimized devices.

D.zh: 錯誤。Direct Connect 提供穩定的網路連接，但遷移 20 TB 的資料可能需要很長時間，特別是當遷移需要在兩週內完成時。這種方法可能無法滿足時間要求且成本可能很高。
D.en: Incorrect. Direct Connect provides a stable network connection, but migrating 20 TB of data may take a long time, especially when the migration is required to be completed within two weeks. This method may not meet time requirements and may be costly.

answer_text.zh: A. 訂購 AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized 設備。將 AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) 與 AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) 結合使用來遷移資料庫並複製持續變更。將 Snowball Edge 裝置傳送到 AWS 以完成遷移並繼續正在進行的複製。
answer_text.en: A. Order an AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized device. Use AWS Database Migration Service (AWS DMS) with AWS Schema Conversion Tool (AWS SCT) to migrate the database with replication of ongoing changes. Send the Snowball Edge device to AWS to finish the migration and continue the ongoing replication.

discussion.zh: 社群投票原始顯示：A (88%)。熱門討論與考點摘要：此選項使用 AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized 裝置進行遷移，結合 AWS DMS 和 AWS SCT 進行資料庫遷移和連續複製。 Snowball Edge Storage Optimized 專為大規模資料遷移而設計，支援以低成本高效傳輸 20 TB 資料。此方法適用於需要快速遷移且網路頻寬有限的場景。
discussion.en: Community vote as displayed: A (88%). Popular discussion and key point summary: This option uses an AWS Snowball Edge Storage Optimized device for migration, combining AWS DMS and AWS SCT for database migration and continuous replication. Snowball Edge Storage Optimized is designed for large-scale data migration, supporting the efficient transfer of 20 TB of data at a low cost. This method is suitable for scenarios where rapid migration is required and network bandwidth is limited.
